title: campanula carpatica var. hendersonii
intro: Campanula carpatica var. hendersonii, also known as the white bellflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its bell-shaped white flowers and blue-green foliage. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Campanula carpatica var. hendersonii have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and reducing fever, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to treat various health conditions.
